channel.title: NPR Topics: News
channel.description: NPR news, audio, and podcasts. Coverage of breaking stories, national and world news, politics, business, science, technology, and extended coverage of major national and world events.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Australia plans to strengthen laws banning children from social media
item.description: Observers said on Friday the government is responding to evidence that the ban on young children holding accounts on platforms including Facebook, Instagram and YouTube had failed since it came into force in December year.
item.content:encoded:
Observers said on Friday the government is responding to evidence that the ban on young children holding accounts on platforms including Facebook, Instagram and YouTube had failed since it came into force in December year.

(Image credit: Rick Rycroft)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: South Korean ex-first lady sentenced to 7 years for bribery scandal
item.description: The former first lady had previously been sentenced to four years in a separate case involving the Unification Church.
item.content:encoded:
The former first lady had previously been sentenced to four years in a separate case involving the Unification Church.

(Image credit: Jung Yeon-je)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Rescuers scramble to find Venezuela earthquake survivors. And, SCOTUS rules on asylum
item.description: Rescuers are searching for thousands of people missing after two earthquakes struck Venezuela on Wednesday. And, the Supreme Court says the U.S. can turn away asylum seekers at the border.
item.content:encoded:
Rescuers are searching for thousands of people missing after two earthquakes struck Venezuela on Wednesday. And, the Supreme Court says the U.S. can turn away asylum seekers at the border.

(Image credit: Jesus Vargas)
item.dc:creator: Brittney Melton

item.title: Aid worker says hundreds remain trapped after Venezuela earthquakes
item.description: After earthquakes shattered much of the capital in Venezuela, Project Hope's Cesar Jimenez says rescue crews are racing to save hundreds trapped as international aid arrives and hospitals struggle to cope.
item.content:encoded:
After earthquakes shattered much of the capital in Venezuela, Project Hope's Cesar Jimenez says rescue crews are racing to save hundreds trapped as international aid arrives and hospitals struggle to cope.

(Image credit: Javier Campos)
item.dc:creator: Leila Fadel

item.title: 'We are with you, Venezuela': Houston community rallies after deadly earthquakes
item.description: Venezuelans living in the U.S organize donation drives in response to the devastating earthquakes.
item.content:encoded:
Venezuelans living in the U.S organize donation drives in response to the devastating earthquakes.

(Image credit: Lucio Vasquez)
item.dc:creator: Lucio Vasquez

item.title: How well do you know your Reflecting Pool news? Because the quiz will test you
item.description: This week, the beleaguered body of water faced new woes. Plus soccer, gambling and U.K. politics!
item.content:encoded: This week, the beleaguered body of water faced new woes. Plus soccer, gambling and U.K. politics!
item.dc:creator: Holly J. Morris

item.title: Trump axed a Black history exhibit. Former park rangers are teaching it anyway.
item.description: As the U.S. prepares to celebrate its 250th anniversary, former national park rangers are hosting teach-ins and sharing history that the Trump administration has sought to erase from federal land.
item.content:encoded:
As the U.S. prepares to celebrate its 250th anniversary, former national park rangers are hosting teach-ins and sharing history that the Trump administration has sought to erase from federal land.

(Image credit: KT Kanazawich for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Marissa J. Lang

item.title: Ex-NOAA employees re-create a valuable climate data site shut down by Trump
item.description: Former NOAA staffers have launched a new website that provides climate information. It replaces a government site that was shut down when the Trump administration took office.
item.content:encoded:
Former NOAA staffers have launched a new website that provides climate information. It replaces a government site that was shut down when the Trump administration took office.

(Image credit: Chandan Khanna)
item.dc:creator: Scott Neuman

item.title: Morning news brief
item.description: Rescuers in Venezuela continue search for the missing after devastating earthquakes, SCOTUS rulings give Trump more power to set immigration policy, Trump works to woo struggling American farmers.
item.content:encoded: Rescuers in Venezuela continue search for the missing after devastating earthquakes, SCOTUS rulings give Trump more power to set immigration policy, Trump works to woo struggling American farmers.
item.dc:creator: Michel Martin

item.title: In a first since Trump deployed the guard to D.C., Democrats are sending troops
item.description: Several Democratic governors have sent National Guard troops to Washington, D.C., ahead of America 250, amid President Trump's highly visible — and controversial — ongoing deployment to the city.
item.content:encoded:
Several Democratic governors have sent National Guard troops to Washington, D.C., ahead of America 250, amid President Trump's highly visible — and controversial — ongoing deployment to the city.

(Image credit: Rahmat Gul)
item.dc:creator: Kat Lonsdorf
